summary: Re-enabled the decompiled SysDolphin baselib LObj object in the build by switching obj_files.mk from the assembly fallback object to the C source object for lobj.

key_files.reason: Updated TEXT_O_FILES to build src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c.o instead of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.s.o.

what_changed: In obj_files.mk, the TEXT_O_FILES entry for LObj changed from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.s.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c.o.
The PR contains only this one-line build list change, indicating that lobj.c was considered ready to replace the assembly object in the linked text objects.
smart_moves: Kept the change minimal: only the object-file selection was changed, which makes the PR easy to audit and isolates any build or matching impact to LObj.
Used the standard decomp progression pattern of replacing an asm/*.s.o fallback with the corresponding src/*.c.o once the C translation is match-ready.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled file is ready, obj_files.mk must be updated from the asm object path to the src object path for that module to count in the linked build.
A successful enablement PR can be intentionally tiny if the matching work already exists elsewhere; the important evidence is the obj_files.mk swap from assembly to C.
For SysDolphin baselib files, preserve the existing TEXT_O_FILES ordering and change only the specific object path being re-enabled.
naming_conventions: The module name is lowercase in the build path: lobj, while the PR title refers to the conceptual object as LObj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR used the standard matching tactic of replacing lobj.s.o with lobj.c.o in TEXT_O_FILES, implying the C file was intended to match sufficiently for the build.
No diff evidence is provided for the underlying lobj.c matching techniques; this PR only records the build-list re-enable step.
follow_up_queries: Find the PR or commit that introduced or matched src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c before PR1171.
Search later PRs touching sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c for any regressions or naming cleanups after LObj was re-enabled.
Compare nearby obj_files.mk entries for mobj, aobj, cobj, fobj, and pobj to understand baselib decompilation progress around this PR.
